basic_info.incident_name_cn: 托斯科阿冯炼油厂火灾事故

process_context.operating_conditions: 高温高压常减压蒸馏工况；涉及含硫腐蚀性介质，长期运行导致设备壁厚减薄

failure_mechanism.root_cause: Inadequate corrosion control and failure to identify thinning in a naphtha line during nonroutine maintenance planning, leading to catastrophic rupture under operating pressure.
failure_mechanism.causal_chain: Inadequate corrosion monitoring → Undetected wall thinning in naphtha transfer line → Loss of mechanical integrity during maintenance activity → Sudden rupture → Release of flammable hydrocarbons → Ignition → Fireball and flash fire
failure_mechanism.physical_principles: Corrosion fatigue, pressure containment failure, vapor cloud ignition dynamics, thermal radiation propagation

protection_system.safeguard_failures: LOTO was not properly applied because the line was incorrectly assumed to be isolated; corrosion inspection data was not integrated into maintenance planning; no independent verification of isolation integrity prior to work.
csb_recommendations: Implement rigorous management of change (MOC) for all nonroutine maintenance activities
Establish comprehensive corrosion control program with risk-based inspection and thickness monitoring
Require positive verification of isolation (e.g., double block and bleed, physical disconnect) before hazardous maintenance

embedding_text: The Tosco Avon incident involved a catastrophic rupture of a corroded naphtha transfer line in a crude fractionation unit. Under high-temperature, high-pressure distillation conditions, undetected wall thinning led to sudden loss of containment. Flammable naphtha released, ignited, and caused a fatal fireball. Root cause was failure in corrosion control and hazardous maintenance isolation verification, resulting in four fatalities and one critical injury during nonroutine work.
